heroHeadline: CJNG Crisis Day 59 — Tourist Site Security Overhaul Ordered, Morena Consolidates INE Control
heroSubtitle: Mexico ordered a nationwide security overhaul at all archaeological and tourist sites on April 22 — 50 days before the FIFA World Cup opening — following the April 20 Teotihuacan pyramid shooting that killed a Canadian tourist and injured 13 others. Security Secretary García Harfuch reinforced National Guard deployments and access controls across heritage sites while FIFA expressed full confidence in Mexico as host. Separately, the Chamber of Deputies approved three Morena-aligned INE counselors (334–127 vote), extending the ruling coalition's influence over electoral oversight through 2035 and drawing opposition accusations of 'kidnapping democracy.' CJNG succession crisis enters day 59; El Mayo sentencing remains set for May 18; USMCA formal round scheduled May 25.
footerNote: Aggregated intelligence from all Mexico-based Watchboard trackers. Data sourced from SEDENA, SESNSP, InSight Crime, OHCHR, and major international outlets.
